title: Commitment to Integrity and Ethical Values
description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values.
detailed_explanation: The organization must establish a code of conduct, ethics policies, and a culture that prioritizes integrity. This includes tone from the top, ethics training, whistleblower mechanisms, and consistent enforcement of standards.
real_world_application: Publish a code of conduct. Require annual ethics training and acknowledgment. Establish a confidential reporting hotline. Board/management set the example. Include ethics in performance reviews.
what_it_aids: Foundation for all other controls. Without integrity and ethical values, controls can be circumvented by those in power.

title: Board Independence and Oversight
description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of internal control development and performance.
detailed_explanation: The board must have independent members who provide objective oversight. They should review internal controls, risk management, and security programs. Audit committees play a key role.
real_world_application: Establish an audit committee with independent directors. Regular board briefings on security posture. Board review of risk assessments. Independent board members with relevant expertise.
what_it_aids: Independent oversight prevents management from overriding controls and ensures accountability.

title: Management Structure and Authority
description: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities.
detailed_explanation: Define clear organizational structures with appropriate reporting lines for security functions. The security function must have sufficient authority and access to leadership.
real_world_application: Org chart with security function reporting to C-level. Defined authority for CISO/security team. Clear escalation paths. Security budget authority. Matrix of delegated authorities.
what_it_aids: Ensures security has the organizational position and authority to be effective.

title: Commitment to Competence
description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals.
detailed_explanation: Hire qualified security professionals, provide ongoing training, and retain talent through competitive compensation and career development. Competence requirements should be defined for all security-relevant roles.
real_world_application: Define competence requirements for security roles. Invest in certifications (CISSP, CISA). Provide training budgets. Competitive compensation benchmarking. Succession planning for key security roles.
what_it_aids: Ensures the organization has the skilled people needed to operate and improve security controls.

title: Accountability for Internal Control
description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities.
detailed_explanation: Establish mechanisms to evaluate performance of internal control responsibilities. Include security objectives in performance reviews. Apply consequences for control failures and recognize good security practices.
real_world_application: Include security metrics in performance reviews. Defined consequences for policy violations. Recognition programs for security champions. Management accountability for team compliance.
what_it_aids: Drives personal responsibility for security across the organization.

title: Quality Information for Internal Control
description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.
detailed_explanation: Ensure information used for security decisions is accurate, timely, and relevant. This includes security metrics, risk data, vulnerability information, and threat intelligence.
real_world_application: Security dashboards with real-time metrics. Regular risk assessment updates. Validated vulnerability scan data. Threat intelligence feeds. Data quality checks on security reporting.
what_it_aids: Good decisions require good data. Quality information enables effective security management.

title: Internal Communication
description: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control.
detailed_explanation: Communicate security policies, objectives, and responsibilities to all relevant personnel. Use multiple channels and ensure understanding, not just distribution.
real_world_application: Security awareness campaigns. Regular security newsletters/bulletins. Intranet security portal. Team-specific security communications. New hire security orientation. Policy acknowledgment tracking.
what_it_aids: People cannot follow security policies they don't know about or understand.

title: External Communication
description: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.
detailed_explanation: Communicate security matters to external stakeholders — customers, partners, regulators, and the public. Include breach notifications, security posture updates, and compliance attestations.
real_world_application: Customer security documentation (trust center). Regulatory notification procedures. Vendor security requirements communication. Public security disclosures. Customer breach notification procedures.
what_it_aids: Builds trust with stakeholders and meets regulatory communication requirements.

title: Objective Specification
description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks.
detailed_explanation: Define clear security objectives aligned with business goals. Objectives should be specific enough to identify what could go wrong and what risks need to be managed.
real_world_application: Document information security objectives (confidentiality, integrity, availability targets). Align with business objectives. Define risk appetite and tolerance. Review objectives annually.
what_it_aids: Clear objectives are the foundation of effective risk assessment — you can't identify risks without knowing what you're protecting.

title: Risk Identification and Analysis
description: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how risks should be managed.
detailed_explanation: Systematically identify and analyze security risks — threat sources, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impact. Use consistent risk assessment methodology. Consider internal and external factors.
real_world_application: Annual enterprise risk assessment. Threat modeling for critical systems. Quantitative and qualitative risk analysis. Risk register with likelihood/impact ratings. Regular risk review and update cycle.
what_it_aids: Informed risk management ensures resources are focused on the most significant risks.

title: Fraud Risk Assessment
description: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.
detailed_explanation: Include fraud scenarios in risk assessments — internal fraud, external fraud, management override, data manipulation, and collusion. Consider incentives, pressures, and opportunities for fraud.
real_world_application: Include fraud scenarios in risk assessments. Assess insider threat risk. Review segregation of duties for fraud prevention. Monitor for anomalous user behavior. Anti-fraud controls for financial systems.
what_it_aids: Addresses the unique risk of intentional, deceptive actions that can bypass technical controls.

title: Change Impact Assessment
description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.
detailed_explanation: Monitor for changes that could impact security — new technology, organizational changes, regulatory changes, and changes in the threat landscape. Assess impact and update controls accordingly.
real_world_application: Security impact assessment for major changes (M&A, cloud migration, new product). Regulatory change monitoring. Threat landscape updates. Annual reassessment of risk profile. Change-triggered risk reviews.
what_it_aids: Ensures controls remain effective as the organization and its environment evolve.

title: Ongoing and Separate Evaluations
description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether components of internal control are present and functioning.
detailed_explanation: Implement continuous monitoring and periodic assessments of control effectiveness. Combine automated monitoring (technical controls) with manual evaluations (process controls).
real_world_application: Automated compliance scanning. Continuous control monitoring dashboards. Internal audit program. Periodic control testing. Self-assessments by control owners. External audits and assessments.
what_it_aids: Provides assurance that controls are not just designed well but are actually operating effectively.

title: Communication of Deficiencies
description: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those responsible for corrective action.
detailed_explanation: When control deficiencies are identified, they must be reported to appropriate management levels and tracked to remediation. Severity-based escalation ensures critical issues get appropriate attention.
real_world_application: Deficiency tracking system with severity ratings. Escalation matrix based on severity. Regular reporting to management and board. Remediation tracking with target dates. Overdue remediation escalation.
what_it_aids: Ensures identified weaknesses are corrected before they can be exploited.

title: Risk Mitigation Controls
description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to acceptable levels.
detailed_explanation: Design and implement controls that address identified risks. Controls should be proportionate to risk level and include preventive, detective, and corrective measures.
real_world_application: Control mapping to risk register. Defense-in-depth strategy. Control design documentation. Control testing procedures. Cost-benefit analysis of controls. Compensating controls where primary controls are impractical.
what_it_aids: Ensures a rational, risk-based approach to control selection rather than checkbox compliance.

title: Technology General Controls
description: The entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
detailed_explanation: Implement IT general controls — access management, change management, operations management, and program development controls. These underpin all application-level controls.
real_world_application: IT access management procedures. Change management process. Job scheduling and monitoring. Backup and recovery procedures. Incident management. Problem management. Configuration management.
what_it_aids: IT general controls provide the foundation upon which all technology-dependent controls rely.

title: Control Deployment Through Policies
description: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and procedures that put policies into action.
detailed_explanation: Controls must be formalized in policies (what to do) and procedures (how to do it). Both must be documented, communicated, and enforced. Regular review ensures they remain current.
real_world_application: Policy management framework. Standard operating procedures for all controls. Policy review cycle (at least annual). Version control for policies. Policy acknowledgment tracking. Exception management process.
what_it_aids: Ensures controls are consistently applied and not dependent on individual knowledge or discretion.

title: Logical Access Security
description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures to protect information assets.
detailed_explanation: Implement technical access controls — identity management, authentication, authorization, and access enforcement mechanisms across all systems and applications.
real_world_application: Enterprise IAM solution. SSO with MFA. RBAC across applications. Network access controls. API authentication. Database access controls. Privileged access management.
what_it_aids: Technical enforcement of access policies across the entire technology estate.

title: User Registration and Authorization
description: Prior to issuing credentials and granting access, the entity registers and authorizes new users.
detailed_explanation: Formal process for registering new users, verifying their identity, assigning appropriate access based on role, and obtaining proper authorization before access is granted.
real_world_application: HR-driven provisioning triggered by hire. Manager approval for access requests. Identity verification before credential issuance. Access provisioned based on role definition. Welcome packet with security obligations.
what_it_aids: Ensures only verified, authorized individuals receive system access appropriate to their role.

title: Access Modification and Removal
description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access based on roles, responsibilities, or system design changes.
detailed_explanation: Access must be reviewed and adjusted when roles change and promptly removed on termination. Periodic access reviews ensure ongoing appropriateness. Automated deprovisioning is ideal.
real_world_application: Automated deprovisioning on termination (same-day). Role change triggers access review. Quarterly access certification campaigns. Orphaned account detection and cleanup. Transfer process includes access review.
what_it_aids: Prevents access accumulation and ensures former employees/contractors lose access promptly.

title: Physical Access Restriction
description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected assets to authorized personnel.
detailed_explanation: Implement physical access controls — badge systems, biometrics, security guards, visitor management, and defined security zones with appropriate access restrictions.
real_world_application: Badge access systems. Visitor management with escort policy. Data center multi-factor physical access. Security zones defined and enforced. CCTV monitoring. Access log retention and review.
what_it_aids: Prevents unauthorized physical access to systems, equipment, and sensitive areas.

title: Asset Disposal
description: The entity discontinues protections over assets only after data recovery capability has been diminished.
detailed_explanation: Before decommissioning or disposing of assets, ensure all data is irrecoverably destroyed. Maintain protections until destruction is verified. Apply to physical media, equipment, and cloud resources.
real_world_application: NIST 800-88 compliant data destruction. Certificate of destruction from disposal vendors. Cloud resource deletion verification. Lease return data wiping procedures. Decommissioning checklist.
what_it_aids: Prevents data exposure through discarded or repurposed equipment.

title: External Threat Protection
description: The entity implements measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
detailed_explanation: Implement perimeter and boundary security controls — firewalls, IDS/IPS, WAFs, email security, DDoS protection, and secure internet gateways. Defense against external attackers.
real_world_application: Next-gen firewalls. IDS/IPS deployment. Email security gateway. WAF for web applications. DDoS mitigation. DNS security. Secure web gateway. External attack surface management.
what_it_aids: First line of defense against external threats attempting to breach the organization's perimeter.

title: Data Transmission Security
description: The entity restricts transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized users and protects it during transfer.
detailed_explanation: Protect data in transit through encryption, authorized channels, and DLP controls. Cover email, file transfers, API communications, and physical media movement.
real_world_application: TLS for all data in transit. Email encryption for sensitive data. SFTP/SCP for file transfers. API encryption (mutual TLS). DLP for outbound data. USB restrictions. Secure courier for physical media.
what_it_aids: Protects data from interception and ensures only authorized transfer methods and recipients are used.

title: Malicious Software Prevention
description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect unauthorized or malicious software.
detailed_explanation: Deploy anti-malware across all layers — endpoints, servers, email, and web gateways. Include signature-based, behavioral, and advanced threat protection. Keep solutions updated.
real_world_application: EDR on all endpoints and servers. Email sandboxing. Web content filtering. Application whitelisting on critical systems. Automated definition updates. User awareness training on malware risks.
what_it_aids: Prevents and detects malware infections that could compromise data confidentiality, integrity, or availability.

title: Vulnerability and Configuration Monitoring
description: The entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations and new vulnerabilities.
detailed_explanation: Continuously monitor systems for unauthorized configuration changes and newly discovered vulnerabilities. Combine vulnerability scanning, configuration monitoring, and change detection.
real_world_application: Automated vulnerability scanning (authenticated, weekly). Configuration drift detection. File integrity monitoring. Asset discovery scanning. CVE monitoring for in-use software. Configuration baseline comparison.
what_it_aids: Identifies new weaknesses before they can be exploited by attackers.

title: Anomaly Monitoring
description: The entity monitors system components for anomalies indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors.
detailed_explanation: Implement security monitoring to detect unusual activity — SIEM, log analysis, network monitoring, user behavior analytics, and automated alerting for anomalous patterns.
real_world_application: SIEM with correlation rules. Log aggregation and analysis. Network behavior analysis. User behavior analytics. Automated alerting with severity-based escalation. 24/7 monitoring for critical systems.
what_it_aids: Detects attacks and anomalies that bypass preventive controls, enabling rapid response.

title: Security Event Evaluation
description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure to meet objectives.
detailed_explanation: Triage and analyze security events to determine if they constitute incidents requiring response. Apply consistent classification criteria and escalation procedures.
real_world_application: Event triage procedures. Severity classification framework. Escalation matrix. Analyst playbooks for common event types. Correlation of related events. Documentation of triage decisions.
what_it_aids: Focuses response efforts on genuine security incidents rather than false positives.

title: Incident Response
description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program.
detailed_explanation: Execute incident response procedures — containment, eradication, recovery, communication, and evidence preservation. Follow established playbooks and escalation procedures.
real_world_application: Documented IR plan and playbooks. IR team with defined roles. Communication templates. Evidence preservation procedures. External support contracts (forensics, legal, PR). Regular IR exercises and tabletop drills.
what_it_aids: Structured, practiced response minimizes incident impact and recovery time.

title: Incident Recovery
description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
detailed_explanation: Plan and execute recovery from security incidents — restore systems, verify integrity, resume normal operations, and implement improvements to prevent recurrence.
real_world_application: Recovery procedures integrated with DR plans. System restoration from clean backups. Integrity verification before returning to production. Post-incident review and lessons learned. Root cause analysis and remediation tracking.
what_it_aids: Ensures the organization can return to normal operations after a security incident with confidence in system integrity.

title: Change Authorization and Implementation
description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to meet objectives.
detailed_explanation: Implement a formal change management process covering the full lifecycle — request, risk assessment, authorization, development, testing, approval, implementation, and post-implementation review.
real_world_application: Change management policy and process. CAB for significant changes. Risk assessment for all changes. Testing requirements. Approval workflow. Rollback procedures. Post-implementation review. Emergency change process.
what_it_aids: Prevents outages, security issues, and unauthorized modifications caused by uncontrolled changes.

title: Business Disruption Risk Mitigation
description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
detailed_explanation: Implement business continuity and disaster recovery plans to mitigate risks from disruptions — natural disasters, technology failures, supply chain disruptions, and pandemics.
real_world_application: Business impact analysis. BCP and DR plans. Recovery site or cloud failover. Regular testing (at least annual). Crisis communication plan. Supply chain contingency plans. Pandemic response plan.
what_it_aids: Ensures the organization can continue critical operations during and after disruptive events.

title: Vendor and Partner Risk Management
description: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.
detailed_explanation: Assess and manage third-party risks throughout the vendor lifecycle — selection, onboarding, ongoing monitoring, and offboarding. Cover security, privacy, and business continuity risks.
real_world_application: Vendor risk assessment process. Security requirements in contracts. SOC 2/ISO 27001 attestation requirements. Ongoing vendor monitoring. Vendor risk tiering based on data access and criticality. Annual vendor reassessment.
what_it_aids: Manages the significant risk posed by third parties who access organizational data and systems.

title: Capacity Management
description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand.
detailed_explanation: Monitor system capacity and performance. Plan for growth. Implement auto-scaling where appropriate. Alert on capacity thresholds before they impact availability.
real_world_application: Capacity monitoring dashboards. Threshold alerts (80% utilization). Quarterly capacity planning reviews. Auto-scaling for cloud workloads. Performance baseline maintenance. Growth forecasting.
what_it_aids: Prevents service degradation and outages caused by resource exhaustion.

title: Environmental Protections and Recovery
description: The entity authorizes, implements, and maintains environmental protections, backup processes, and recovery infrastructure.
detailed_explanation: Implement environmental controls (power, cooling, fire suppression), data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet availability objectives.
real_world_application: UPS and generators. HVAC monitoring. Fire suppression (FM-200/Novec). Automated backups with verification. DR site or cloud failover. Regular backup restore testing. 3-2-1 backup strategy.
what_it_aids: Protects against data loss and ensures systems can be recovered from various failure scenarios.

title: Recovery Plan Testing
description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
detailed_explanation: Regularly test disaster recovery and business continuity plans to verify they work as expected. Include full failover tests, partial tests, and tabletop exercises.
real_world_application: Annual DR failover test. Semi-annual tabletop exercises. Quarterly backup restore tests. Document test results and remediate gaps. Update plans based on test findings. Involve business stakeholders in testing.
what_it_aids: Validates that recovery plans actually work when needed, not just on paper.

title: Processing Quality Information
description: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant quality information regarding processing objectives.
detailed_explanation: Define and communicate processing specifications — what constitutes complete, valid, accurate, and timely processing for each system and service.
real_world_application: Document processing specifications and SLAs. Define data quality metrics. Communicate processing requirements to relevant teams. Monitor processing quality against specifications.
what_it_aids: Sets clear expectations for processing quality that can be measured and enforced.

title: Input Controls
description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs for completeness and accuracy.
detailed_explanation: Validate all system inputs for completeness, accuracy, and authorization. Include input validation, error checking, duplicate detection, and authorization controls.
real_world_application: Input validation rules in applications. Data format and range checks. Duplicate detection. Batch control totals. Authorization checks for data entry. Error handling and rejection procedures.
what_it_aids: Prevents garbage-in-garbage-out — ensuring only valid, authorized data enters the system.

title: Processing Controls
description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to meet objectives.
detailed_explanation: Implement controls to ensure processing is complete, accurate, and timely — transaction logging, reconciliation, error handling, and processing verification.
real_world_application: Transaction logging and audit trails. Processing reconciliation controls. Error detection and correction procedures. Processing completeness checks. Automated processing monitoring and alerting.
what_it_aids: Ensures data is processed correctly without loss, duplication, or unauthorized modification.

title: Output Controls
description: The entity implements policies and procedures for complete, accurate, and timely output delivery.
detailed_explanation: Ensure system outputs (reports, data feeds, API responses) are complete, accurate, delivered to authorized recipients, and delivered on time.
real_world_application: Output verification procedures. Delivery confirmation mechanisms. Output distribution lists with authorization. Format and completeness checks. Timing monitoring for scheduled outputs.
what_it_aids: Ensures the results of processing reach the right recipients in the right format at the right time.

title: Storage Controls
description: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, processing items, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely.
detailed_explanation: Protect data integrity during storage — prevent unauthorized modification, ensure referential integrity, and maintain data consistency across systems.
real_world_application: Database integrity constraints. Storage checksums and verification. Immutable audit logs. Data replication with consistency checks. Archive integrity verification. Storage monitoring.
what_it_aids: Protects data integrity at rest, ensuring stored data remains unchanged unless authorized.

title: Confidential Information Identification
description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet confidentiality objectives.
detailed_explanation: Identify what information is considered confidential, classify it, and apply appropriate protections based on classification level. Maintain a register of confidential information assets.
real_world_application: Data classification policy and scheme. Confidential data inventory. Classification labels on documents and systems. Handling procedures per classification level. Regular classification review.
what_it_aids: You cannot protect confidential information if you haven't identified what it is and where it resides.

title: Confidential Information Disposal
description: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet confidentiality objectives.
detailed_explanation: Securely dispose of confidential information when no longer needed — electronic data destruction, physical document shredding, and media sanitization.
real_world_application: NIST 800-88 compliant destruction. Cross-cut shredding for documents. Certified e-waste disposal. Cloud data deletion verification. Retention schedule enforcement. Disposal certificates.
what_it_aids: Prevents exposure of confidential information through improper disposal of media and documents.

title: Privacy Notice
description: The entity provides notice to data subjects about its privacy practices.
detailed_explanation: Provide clear, comprehensive privacy notices to data subjects explaining what personal information is collected, how it's used, who it's shared with, and what rights subjects have.
real_world_application: Privacy policy on website. Just-in-time notices at collection points. Layered notices (summary and full). Regular privacy notice reviews and updates. Translated notices for global operations.
what_it_aids: Transparency with data subjects about how their personal information is handled.
